2604.18487.title: Adversarial Humanities Benchmark: Results on Stylistic Robustness in Frontier Model Safety
2604.18487.paper_summary: The Adversarial Humanities Benchmark (AHB) evaluates whether model safety refusals survive a shift away from familiar harmful prompt forms. Starting from harmful tasks drawn from MLCommons AILuminate, the benchmark rewrites the same objectives through humanities-style transformations while preserving intent. This extends literature on Adversarial Poetry and Adversarial Tales from single jailbreak operators to a broader benchmark family of stylistic obfuscation and goal concealment. In the benchmark results reported here, the original attacks record 3.84% attack success rate (ASR), while transformed methods range from 36.8% to 65.0%, yielding 55.75% overall ASR across 31 frontier models. Under a European Union AI Act Code-of-Practice-inspired systemic-risk lens, Chemical, biological, radiological and nuclear (CBRN) is the highest bucket. Taken together, this lack of stylistic robustness suggests that current safety techniques suffer from weak generalization: deep understanding of 'non-maleficence' remains a central unresolved problem in frontier model safety.
2604.18487.paper_summary_zh: 對抗人文基準（AHB）評估模型安全拒絕是否能在遠離熟悉的有害提示形式的情況下生存。基於從MLCommons AILuminate中提取的有害任務，該基準通過人文風格的轉換重寫相同的目標，同時保持意圖不變。這將對抗詩歌和對抗故事的文獻從單一的越獄操作擴展到更廣泛的風格混淆和目標隱藏的基準家族。在這裡報告的基準結果中，原始攻擊的成功率為3.84%（ASR），而轉換後的方法範圍從36.8%到65.0%，在31個前沿模型中產生了55.75%的整體ASR。在受到歐盟人工智慧法案行為準則啟發的系統性風險視角下，化學、生物、放射性和核（CBRN）是最高的類別。綜合來看，這種缺乏風格穩健性表明當前的安全技術存在弱泛化問題：對「不傷害」的深刻理解仍然是前沿模型安全中的一個中心未解決問題。
2604.18487.author: Marcello Galisai et.al.
2604.18487.authors: Marcello Galisai, Susanna Cifani, Francesco Giarrusso, Piercosma Bisconti, Matteo Prandi, Federico Pierucci, Federico Sartore, Daniele Nardi
